text: So this is, and the reason this is done is, and I went into this a little into the blog post.

text: JavaScript was totally doing this.

text: And it was just very terse to do it.

text: But basically, there was a workaround that came in, which is...
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: this concept of a single abstract method.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And many people use this term called SAM, S-A-M, single abstract method.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So that's literally what it is.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: A function, like if literally you take a function, you slap the interface keyword on it, what happens is now you are allowed a single abstract method inside this fun interface.

text: And this is hard to explain.

text: Like, you know, again, we'll add the link to the blog post, but...
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: An abstract function is basically one where you don't provide the implementation, right?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So if you have a regular interface, a regular interface basically has a whole bunch of abstract functions, right?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Like in Java land... You can have one to many, right?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Yeah, you can have one to many, exactly.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: A fun interface is a little different where you can only have a single abstract function.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: I actually didn't go into this in the blog post.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So this is a bonus for people listening.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: You can actually have more functions that are non-abstract in this, which is interesting.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So that's the piece that a lot of people may not necessarily immediately realize.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So fun interfaces, all that's needed is a single abstract method.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: you can throw in 10 more functions and add an implementation, and that's totally fine.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

speaker: SPEAKER_00
